concepts.name: API response cache via Redis
concepts.summary: Cache API responses in the existing Redis instance with a 60-second TTL so all three load-balanced replicas serve consistent cached results.
reasoning: Initially considered an in-memory LRU cache but pivoted after the user pointed out the service runs three replicas behind a load balancer, which would cause inconsistent responses across replicas.
Chose Redis because it shares cache state across all replicas and is already part of the operational footprint (session store), so no new infra dependency is introduced.
Selected a 60-second TTL to balance response freshness against the load-reduction goal.
